element.name: Copper
element.summary: Copper is a chemical element; it has symbol Cu and atomic number 29. It is a soft, malleable, and ductile metal with very high thermal and electrical conductivity. A freshly exposed surface of pure copper has a pinkish-orange color. Copper is used as a conductor of heat and electricity, as a building material, and as a constituent of various metal alloys, such as sterling silver used in jewelry, cupronickel used to make marine hardware and coins, and constantan used in strain gauges and thermocouples for temperature measurement.
element.etymologyDescription: Latin cuprum: from Cyprus, where it was mined

group.label: Coinage metals
group.description: Copper, silver, gold, and roentgenium form group 11 -- the coinage metals. Their malleability, lustre, and resistance to oxidation have made them symbols of value for millennia.
